First, switch back to main branch and then identify the commit for the production config which is at position HEAD@{4}. Then, we can use git reset to restore this commit. However, if we want to keep changes from the current main branch and just recover the production config files, we can use git checkout. Alternatively, we could use git cherry-pick to apply the specific commit without changing the branch position.
